subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: rebuilt from independent sources (IMF/World Bank where available, V-Dem, RSF, Freedom House, HRW, Amnesty, ACLED, UN/OHCHR); both rival authorities excluded as governance sources.

De-facto partitioned since 2014 (a Tripoli-based government in the west vs the Haftar/LNA-aligned east) with rival governments and a contested central bank. Data is fragmentary; figures are estimates.
Standout drags: a divided militia-ruled petrostate, documented migrant enslavement, periodic warfare, and a resource curse fuelling the conflict. Overall: clearly negative.

economic.notes: Oil-revenue-rich but contested and looted between factions, with a contested central bank.

fiscal_state.notes: Oil-revenue-rich but with no unified budget; spending split and looted between factions. (IMF Art IV 2025; World Bank)

monetary_independence.notes: A nominally-reunified but still faction-contested central bank, a 2025 devaluation, and eastern off-books printing. (IMF; WPR)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The dinar has no international role. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Moderate but rising post-devaluation, with contested data. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: A split, cash-based, embargo-exposed, fragile system. (IMF; World Bank)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A ~95% oil-export rentier repeatedly halted by blockades and faction fights - a resource curse fuelling the war. (World Bank; CRS)

institutional.notes: No unified state, militia rule, collapsed data, and a poor press environment.

rule_of_law.notes: No unified state, militia rule, and no judicial independence - a closed-autocracy environment. (V-Dem 2025; Freedom House; HRW)

statistical_integrity.notes: Collapsed and contested across dual institutions - external estimates only. (IMF caveats)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Split and militia-captured, with parallel administrations. (World Bank; ICG)

press_freedom.notes: A poor RSF standing, with militias targeting journalists. (RSF 2025; CPJ)

political_social.notes: Rival governments and periodic warfare, with documented migrant enslavement.

political_stability.notes: Rival governments since 2014, 2025 Tripoli clashes, and no unified elections - a frozen/active conflict. (ACLED; ICG)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Documented migrant enslavement, torture, and detention - among the worst. (UN/OHCHR; US TIP)

social_cohesion.notes: Fragmented along east-west, regional, and tribal lines. (ICG)

demographics.notes: Young with high oil-wealth-per-capita, but war-disrupted jobs. (UN; World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A fragmented proxy battleground with foreign-mercenary presence.

alliance_reliability.notes: A fragmented proxy arena - the east Russia/Egypt/UAE-backed, the west Turkey-backed. (CSIS; Atlantic Council)

conflict_involvement.notes: Divided with periodic violence and a Russian-mercenary presence. (ISW; Atlantic Council)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: A UN arms embargo, frozen-asset legacy, and contested oil revenue. (UNSC)

physical_practical.notes: Desert heat and a catastrophic 2023 flood, atop war-damaged infrastructure.

climate.notes: Extreme aridity/heat plus Derna-2023-style flash-flood risk, with conflict-collapsed adaptive governance despite oil. (ND-GAIN; Germanwatch CRI)

infrastructure.notes: Oil-funded but war-damaged, with the Derna flood exposing neglect. (World Bank; HRW)

summary: A de-facto partitioned petrostate, divided since 2014 between a Tripoli-based government in the west and a Haftar/LNA-aligned east, with rival governments, a contested central bank, and oil revenue (~95% of exports) that is repeatedly halted by blockades and looted between factions - a resource curse that fuels the conflict. Militia rule means no judicial independence, documented migrant enslavement is among the world's worst, and periodic warfare persists amid a foreign-mercenary presence. Extreme heat and the catastrophic 2023 Derna flood weigh on the physical side. Data is fragmentary; the picture is clearly negative.
